sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Juventus are in excellent form, unbeaten in their last 7 matches, with convincing wins like a 5-0 thrashing of Cremonese. Cagliari, on the other hand, have struggled recently, failing to win in their last 3 games and conceding in all of their last 6 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Juventus have dominated Cagliari in recent encounters, winning 3 of the last 5 head-to-heads and remaining unbeaten in the last 10 meetings. Their latest clash in November 2025 ended with a 2-1 Juventus victory at home.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Juventus, sitting 4th with 39 points, boast a strong attack (32 goals scored) and a solid defense (16 conceded). Cagliari, in 16th with 19 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 30 goals, and lack potency in the final third with low penalty area entries and final third passes.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Kenan Yıldız has been a standout for Juventus, with 7 goals and 4 assists this season, consistently performing against Cagliari. For Cagliari, forwards like Gennaro Borrelli and Sebastiano Esposito will need to capitalize on limited chances.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Juventus are likely to control possession (averaging 56% in recent games) and exploit Cagliari’s defensive frailties, especially given Cagliari’s tendency to concede first. Cagliari may rely on late goals (26% of their goals come after the 76th minute) to mount a comeback.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Juventus’ superior form, head-to-head dominance, and Sofascore’s prediction of a 50% chance of an away win, they are clear favorites. Cagliari’s home struggles and defensive issues make an upset unlikely, though they might score given Juventus’ occasional lapses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Cagliari and Juventus at Unipol Domus ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Juventus dominated possession and territorial play, spending significantly more time in the attacking third compared to Cagliari. Despite their control, they failed to convert their dominance into goals, while Cagliari struggled to create meaningful opportunities on the counter. The match has been intense, with both teams showing moments of intent but lacking the final touch in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Juventus' attacking dominance
halftimeSections.text: Juventus clearly held the upper hand in the first half, with sustained periods of pressure in Cagliari’s half. Their ability to enter the final third and penetrate the penalty area frequently showcased their attacking intent. They managed a few notable attempts on goal, though their finishing let them down. Players like Fabio Miretti showed promise in creating chances, while the team’s overall build-up play was fluid, often pinning Cagliari back. If they can sharpen their finishing, Juventus looks poised to capitalize in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Cagliari's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari, on the other hand, adopted a more defensive approach, focusing on absorbing Juventus’ pressure. Their backline, led by players like Yerry Mina, was kept busy but managed to hold firm under relentless attacks. While their entries into the final third were limited, they showed flashes of potential on the break but lacked composure in key moments. Cagliari will need to remain compact and look for counter-attacking opportunities if they are to turn the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Juventus consistently maintained the upper hand in terms of attacking momentum, with multiple spells of sustained pressure in Cagliari’s territory. They had several peaks of intensity, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half, where they pushed forward with intent. Cagliari, by contrast, struggled to build any significant momentum in attack, with only brief moments of respite from Juventus’ dominance. This imbalance suggests Juventus are more likely to dictate the tempo moving forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key individual performances
halftimeSections.text: For Juventus, Manuel Locatelli stood out with a strong presence in midfield, dictating play and contributing to their attacking build-up. Teun Koopmeiners also showed quality in linking play and winning duels, which helped maintain their control. On Cagliari’s side, Yerry Mina was a rock at the back, clearing danger on multiple occasions, while Luca Mazzitelli offered some stability in midfield with crucial tackles and interceptions. These players will likely play pivotal roles in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the first-half dynamics, Juventus are the favorites to take the lead in the second half if they can convert their territorial dominance into goals. Cagliari will likely continue to sit deep, hoping to frustrate their opponents and strike on the counter. Tactical adjustments from Juventus, possibly involving fresh legs in attack, could unlock Cagliari’s defense. Meanwhile, Cagliari must improve their transition play to pose a threat. The second half could see a breakthrough, especially if Juventus maintain their intensity.
